llm2api.description:
LLM2API — DeepSeek (deepseek-v4-flash) inference, plus crypto pre-trade checks. OpenAI-compatible chat completions API for AI agents and MCP workflows: send a messages array to POST .../llm2api/v1/chat/completions and receive a response from DeepSeek (deepseek-v4-flash). Input capped at 4,000 characters across max 20 messages; output capped at 2,048 tokens.

Skills:
  POST .../llm2api/v1/chat/completions
    OpenAI-compatible chat completion (DeepSeek).

  POST .../llm2api/trade/risk-check
    Crypto token risk check. Body: {"symbol": "BTC"}. Scans recent news for hack/exploit/delisting/rug-pull/lawsuit events, classifies them with confidence scores and returns verdict block|ok. Same pipeline that protects the live Kurage FreqAI trading system (kurage.exbridge.jp/kfreqai.php).

  POST .../llm2api/trade/size-check
    Crypto order size / liquidity check. Body: {"symbol": "DOGE", "order_size_usdt": 5000}. Applies the live system's 0.1%-of-24h-volume liquidity cap and returns max_safe_size_usdt plus a thin-market warning. No LLM, sub-second.

messages.description: Array of message objects with role and content. Roles: system, user, assistant. Total input text across all messages must not exceed 4,000 characters; max 20 messages.

stream.description: Enable streaming response (Server-Sent Events). Default false.

max_tokens.description: Maximum tokens to generate. Capped at 2,048 per request regardless of the value specified.

updf2md.description: Universal Markdown conversion endpoint for MCP tools and AI agents. It converts public PDF URLs or ticker symbols into agent-ready Markdown, returning OCR/layout metadata for PDFs and report context for ticker-based workflows.

mode.description: Optional mode selector: pdf or ticker.

pdf_url.description: Publicly reachable HTTP(S) URL of the PDF document to convert to Markdown.

ticker.description: Ticker, asset symbol, company name, or project name to turn into a Markdown investment report.

filename.description: Optional filename to send to the backend when the URL has no useful PDF name.

summary.description: Short summary returned for ticker-based report generation.

sources.description: Source URLs used for ticker-based report generation.

pdf_type.description: PDF classification such as text_based or scanned.

processing_time_ms.description: Time spent in the backend converter in milliseconds.

uimage.description: URL2AI ERNIE Image API for AI agents and MCP workflows. Generates an image from a direct prompt, text, a public URL, or an X post URL using ERNIE-Image-Turbo.

input_type.description: One of prompt, text, url, or x_post.

prompt.description: Direct image generation prompt. When input_type=prompt, this prompt is sent to the image backend as-is.

text.description: Direct text input for image generation.

url.description: Publicly reachable HTTP(S) URL to summarize into an image prompt.

tweet_url.description: X post URL when using x_post mode.

input_type.description: Resolved input type used for generation.

prompt.description: Final prompt sent to the image backend.

image_base64.description: Generated image bytes encoded as base64.

output_format.description: Image format such as png or jpeg.

oss2api.description:
OSS2API is a multi-skill AI agent gateway that wraps open-source tools into x402-ready paid endpoints. It exposes four skills under a single service — select a skill by appending its path after the service URL.

Skills:
  POST .../oss2api/image/remove-background
    Remove, replace, or blur the background of any image. Powered by imgly/background-removal-js (AGPL-3.0). Supply image_url or image_base64. Use mode=remove (transparent PNG), mode=replace (fill with background_color or background_image_url), or mode=blur (gaussian blur). Returns binary image or base64 JSON when response=json.

  POST .../oss2api/url/analyze
    Fetch a URL and extract structured content: title, description, headings (h1-h3), links, email/URL entities, and plain-text summary. Set format=markdown to get a Markdown rendering instead. Set depth=full for up to 8000 chars of body text.

  POST .../oss2api/url/browse
    Render a page with a real Chromium browser (Playwright) and return a screenshot or extracted text. Use action=screenshot (returns PNG as base64) or action=extract (returns visible text + links). Useful for JavaScript-heavy pages that static fetch cannot read.

  POST .../oss2api/url/scan
    Run a 3-phase security scan on any URL. Phase 1 checks HTTP security headers (CSP, HSTS, X-Frame-Options, Referrer-Policy, Permissions-Policy). Phase 2 does static HTML analysis for XSS sinks (innerHTML, eval, document.write), SQL error exposure, login form issues, SSRF-prone parameters, and external script count. Phase 3 sends findings to an Ollama AI model for additional vulnerability analysis and remediation recommendations. Returns a risk_score (0-100) and categorised findings (critical/high/medium/low/info).

url.description: [url/analyze, url/browse, url/scan] The target URL to fetch, browse, or scan. Must be an absolute http/https URL.

format.description: [url/analyze] Response format: json (default, returns structured fields) or markdown (returns Markdown rendering of the page body).

depth.description: [url/analyze] Content depth: basic (default, summary up to 600 chars) or full (returns up to 8000 chars of body text in content field).

action.description: [url/browse] Browser action: screenshot (default, returns PNG as base64) or extract (returns visible text and links).

wait_until.description: [url/browse] Playwright page load event to wait for: networkidle (default) or load.

image_url.description: [image/remove-background] Publicly reachable HTTP(S) URL of the source image.

image_base64.description: [image/remove-background] Base64-encoded source image (raw base64 or data URL).

mode.description: [image/remove-background] Processing mode: remove (default — transparent background), replace (solid color or image), blur (gaussian blur).

background_color.description: [image/remove-background, mode=replace] CSS color for the replacement background, e.g. #ffffff or rgba(0,0,0,0).

background_image_url.description: [image/remove-background, mode=replace] Public URL of a background image to composite behind the subject.

output_format.description: [image/remove-background] Output image format: png (default), webp, or jpeg.

response.description: [image/remove-background] Response mode: binary (default, raw image bytes) or json (returns {ok, image_base64, content_type}).

ok.description: true on success (url/* and image/* with response=json).

image_base64.description: [image/remove-background, response=json] Processed image encoded as base64.

content_type.description: [image/remove-background, response=json] MIME type of the returned image, e.g. image/png.

headings.description: [url/analyze] List of {tag, text} objects for h1–h3 headings.

links.description: [url/analyze, url/browse] List of {text, href} objects for absolute links found on the page.

summary.description: [url/analyze] Plain-text excerpt of the page body (up to 600 chars). [url/scan] One-sentence AI security assessment.

markdown.description: [url/analyze, format=markdown] Markdown rendering of the page content.

content.description: [url/analyze, depth=full] Full plain-text body up to 8000 chars. [url/browse, action=extract] Visible page text up to 8000 chars.

screenshot.description: [url/browse, action=screenshot] Base64-encoded PNG screenshot of the rendered page.

findings.description: [url/scan] Categorised findings: {critical, high, medium, low, info} — each an array of '[type] detail' strings.

findings_flat.description: [url/scan] All findings as flat '[SEVERITY/type] detail' strings.

actions.description: [url/scan] Recommended remediation actions generated by the AI analysis phase.

fxbrain.description: Kurage FX Brain — 15 FX judgment skills on DeepSeek (deepseek-v4-flash), built from vendored open-source trading intelligence: TradingAgents (Apache-2.0), FinGPT (MIT), ai-hedge-fund (MIT), pinned commits with full attribution. Judgment only: no broker credentials, no order execution. Select a skill by path after the service URL. Skills: POST .../fxbrain/analyze/technical | /analyze/macro | /analyze/sentiment | /analyze/full (all perspectives in one call) | /debate/bull-bear | /decide/trade (BUY/SELL/HOLD) | /assess/risk (approve/reduce/reject) | /decide/portfolio | /review/trade | /fingpt/sentiment | /fingpt/headline | /fingpt/forecast | /fingpt/report | /hedge/news-sentiment | /hedge/portfolio | /finrobot/forecast (FinRobot Apache-2.0 Market Forecaster: next-week move with % range) | /finrobot/report/{income_stmt,balance_sheet,cash_flow,segment_stmt,risk_assessment,competitors,business_highlights,company_description} | /finmem/decide (FinMem MIT layered-memory decision with risk-seeking/averse character) | /finmem/reflect | /market/opportunity-ranking (rank up to 40 pairs by risk-adjusted opportunity in one call; body {pairs:[{pair,technicals,news,flows,positioning}...],global_context}) | /market/flow-ranking | /market/anomaly | /market/margin-risk | /signal/pair/{PAIR} (e.g. /signal/pair/USD_JPY: evidence-bounded watch/wait/avoid signal). Body: {pair (required, e.g. USD_JPY), timeframe, technicals{}, macro{}, news[], position{}, history[], question} — at least one evidence field required. Returns structured JSON with signal/confidence/evidence and upstream attribution.

pair.description: FX pair like USD_JPY or EUR_USD

timeframe.description: e.g. M15, H1, D. Default H1

result.description: Structured judgment: signal/confidence/evidence/invalidation, or vendor output with upstream attribution (repository, commit, license, function)

kcbrain.description: Kurage Crypto Brain — 15 crypto judgment skills on DeepSeek (deepseek-v4-flash), built from vendored open-source trading intelligence: ai-hedge-fund-crypto (MIT), crypto-trading-agents from TradingAgents (Apache-2.0), LLM_trader, pinned commits with full attribution. Judgment only: no exchange credentials, no order execution. Select a skill by path after the service URL. Skills: POST .../kcbrain/analyze/technical | /analyze/onchain | /analyze/sentiment | /analyze/full (all perspectives in one call) | /debate/bull-bear | /decide/trade (BUY/SELL/HOLD) | /assess/risk (approve/reduce/reject) | /decide/portfolio | /review/trade | /market/opportunity-ranking (rank up to 40 pairs by risk-adjusted opportunity in one call; body {pairs:[{symbol,technicals,derivatives,onchain,news}...],global_context}) | /market/flow-ranking | /market/anomaly | /market/liquidation-risk | /vendor/ai-hedge-fund-crypto/portfolio (ai-hedge-fund-crypto MIT portfolio manager synthesis) | /vendor/llm-trader/analyze (LLM_trader decision-gated analysis) | /signal/pair/{SYMBOL} (e.g. /signal/pair/BTC_USDT: evidence-bounded watch/wait/avoid signal). Body: {symbol (required, e.g. BTC_USDT), timeframe, technicals{}, derivatives{}, onchain{}, defi{}, news[], social[], position{}, portfolio{}, history[], question} — at least one evidence field required. Returns structured JSON with signal/confidence/evidence and upstream attribution. Multi-agent chained debates (bull/bear researcher, multi-analyst desks — 3-5 sequential LLM calls) are sold separately on the direct x402 rail: POST https://bittensorman.xyz/kcbrain/vendor/crypto-trading-agents/debate | /vendor/vibe-trading/research | /vendor/helm-agents/consensus ($0.003 each).

symbol.description: Crypto pair like BTC_USDT or ETH_USDT

timeframe.description: e.g. H1, H4, D. Default H1

onchain.description: Exchange flows, active addresses, whale activity

result.description: Structured judgment: signal/confidence/evidence/invalidation, or vendor output with upstream attribution (repository, commit, license, function)

url2brain.description:
Kurage URL2AI Publisher brain — paste a URL and get back a ready-to-post Japanese (or English) announcement plus a full blog article, grounded only in the page's own extracted content. Runs on DeepSeek (deepseek-v4-flash) for paid x402 calls. This is the same brain behind the Kurage URL2AI Publisher web app (https://url2ai.exbridge.jp/). Select a skill by path after the service URL.

Generation skills: POST .../url2brain/analyze/url (fetch+extract title/description/headings/body from a URL) | /generate/announcement (<=280 char announcement from extracted content) | /generate/blog-article (300-600 word blog article from extracted content) | /generate/from-url (convenience endpoint: fetch + both generators in one call — the recommended single-call skill). Body for generate/from-url and analyze/url: {url (required), language (default ja), tone}. Body for generate/announcement and generate/blog-article: {source (required, the object returned by analyze/url), language, tone}.

Publishing skills ($1.00 each — these ACTUALLY POST to Kurage/EXBRIDGE's own accounts; payment is the posting authorization, no credentials needed from the caller): POST .../url2brain/post/bluesky {text, url} → posts to @bittensorman.bsky.social, framed in the Kurage persona | /post/hatena-bookmark {url, comment, tags} → posts to Kurage's Hatena Bookmark, no framing | /post/aixsns {content, author} → posts to aixec.exbridge.jp, framed as bittensorman (developer/business persona) | /post/bludit {title, body_markdown} → posts a full article to Kurage's Bludit blog (kurage.exbridge.jp/blog, url2pub category), framed in the Kurage persona | /post/hatena-blog {title, body_markdown} → posts a full article to bittensorman's Hatena Blog, framed as bittensorman. Typical flow: call generate/from-url first to get announcement/blog_article text, then feed that text into the post/* skill(s) of your choice.

url.description: [analyze/url, generate/from-url, post/hatena-bookmark] Publicly reachable HTTP(S) URL.

source.description: [generate/announcement, generate/blog-article] The object returned in result.source by analyze/url or generate/from-url.

language.description: Output language. Default ja (Japanese); pass en for English.

tone.description: Optional tone hint, e.g. neutral, enthusiastic. Default neutral.

text.description: [post/bluesky] Post text, max 280 chars including the Kurage persona prefix (required).

model.description: deepseek-v4-flash (generation skills) or the posting backend name (post/* skills)

result.description: [analyze/url] {title, description, headings, links, content}. [generate/announcement] {text}. [generate/blog-article] {title, body_markdown}. [generate/from-url] {source, announcement, blog_article}. [post/*] {ok, status, platform, detail} — detail includes the live post URL on success.
